outlook.headline: DRAM demand remains hot, but momentum is cooling slightly
outlook.summary: DRAM is still seeing strong AI, HBM, and next-node spending, with tight supply keeping the backdrop favorable for Micron and the equipment/test ecosystem. But mention momentum is running below the trailing average, so the near-term setup looks more like sustained strength than a fresh acceleration. Watch whether cost pressure and DRAM-scarcity workarounds start to cap content growth outside the AI server segment.

drivers.label: AI and data-center demand remains strong
drivers.detail: Multiple reads point to hot DRAM demand tied to AI servers, HBM, DDR5/LPDDR, and broader data-center buildouts.

drivers.label: Next-gen node and HBM transition continues
drivers.detail: Customers are pushing 1C, 1-gamma, EUV, and 4F-squared/advanced-node investments, supporting continued DRAM capex and tool spending.

drivers.label: Supply remains tight
drivers.detail: Reads repeatedly describe constrained DRAM capacity or tight supply, which keeps the market favorable and supports pricing/activity.

drivers.label: DRAM pricing and test demand stay elevated
drivers.detail: Elevated DRAM pricing and robust test activity suggest the cycle is still healthy even if not accelerating.

drivers.label: Customers are optimizing away from DRAM
drivers.detail: Some designs are trying to reduce DRAM usage or adopt DRAM-less solutions because it is expensive and hard to source.

risks: A quicker-than-expected supply ramp could ease tightness and pressure pricing.
If AI/server demand pauses, the strongest incremental DRAM driver could slow quickly.
DRAM-cost optimization could reduce content growth in non-AI end markets.
